title: PM Lane 320 - Project Miner Temp Power Customer Delivery And Durable Proof Readback Design No-Live Packet
authority_basis: Standing PM blocker authority and repo technical authority, used here to convert Lane 319's storage plan into a future readback contract without admitting runtime reads, routes, or delivery execution.
activation_condition: Lane 319 already defines the future delivery/proof storage plan and dedicated readback requirement.
No Temp Power customer delivery and durable proof readback-design packet exists yet.
The next blocker after lane 319 remains design-only and does not yet justify route or runtime admission.
current_truth: Lane 315 remains the hosted-green runtime baseline, and it still blocks actual delivery execution inside the current customer-preview review slice.
Lane 319 defines the future storage plan and requires a dedicated readback contract before schema or route admission can be considered.

The next bounded design step is readback only: define what the future delivery/proof status read must return, which classifications are allowed, how preview-review lineage and source matching must work, and how readback must prove separation from finance, source writeback, and customer billing delivery.
Runtime reads, registered routes, and hosted delivery/proof behavior remain separate later packets.
Finance/payroll/billing/invoice/accounting outputs and source writeback remain outside this lane.
allowed_actions: Define the future readback route placeholder, status contract, allowed status values, and readback rules for customer delivery and durable proof review.
Preserve visible blocked boundaries for runtime delivery, finance, and source writeback.
not_allowed: No product code, schema, route implementation, or hosted action.
No runtime read admission or persistence execution.
No live or local POST claiming customer delivery or proof recording occurred.
No finance, payroll, billing, invoice, accounting, external finance sync, or source writeback work.
No auth, ingress, DNS, or secret changes.
validation: Decision label, selected outcome, future readback route, allowed status values, readback rules, and blocked-domain boundaries are present across touched lane 320 files
Canonical PM docs/status route the next follow-on step through route and payload design rather than schema or runtime delivery admission
git diff --check passes for touched lane 320 and canonical PM doc surfaces
validation_result: PASS: lane 320 defines the future delivery/proof readback contract and keeps route implementation, schema, runtime delivery, finance, and source-writeback widening deferred.
